I had been feeling guilty about the way I had treated my previous job, and the thought of being rejected once again had me on edge.

I had been applying for a position at a prestigious law firm, and the interviewer had asked me about my previous experience working in a similar capacity. It was a still evening, and the air was cool and crisp. I sat on the grass, staring up at the stars, lost in thought. For a moment, the world around me seemed to slow down, and I felt a sense of calm wash over me. But then, my guilty conscience caught up with me.
As I looked up at the sky, I realized that I had been living a life of constant stress and worry. I had been caught up in the demands of my job, the expectations of my family, and the endless to-do list that seemed to never end. I felt guilty for neglecting my own needs and desires, for not taking time to relax and recharge.

As she sat on her couch, wrapped in her blanket, her mind drifted to the past. The sound of the TV drowned out any noise, and the silence was deafening. She thought back to a time when she had felt guilty, when she had known that something she had done was wrong, and had to come to terms with it.
The memory made her feel a twinge of guilt, a feeling that echoed in her chest like a heavy weight. She closed her eyes, trying to escape the reminder, but it was too strong.
